{"query":"El Salvador life imprisonment constitutional amendment March 2026 crimes eligible murder rape terrorism","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2277,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/americas/el-salvador-reforms-constitution-allow-life-sentences-2026-03-17/","title":"El Salvador reforms constitution to allow for life sentences | Reuters","content":"March 17 (Reuters) - El Salvador's Congress on Tuesday approved a constitutional amendment to allow for ​life sentences for charges including murder, ‌rape, and terrorism, as the government of President Nayib Bukele continues its crackdown on the country's criminal ​gangs.
While Salvadoran courts previously handed down ​sentences exceeding 100 years, the law capped ⁠actual time served at 60 years.
The ​reform passed with the support of 59 ​lawmakers, with one lawmaker voting against."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/4/15/el-salvador-publishes-law-allowing-life-sentences-for-minors-as-young-as-12","title":"El Salvador publishes law allowing life sentences for minors as young as 12 | Crime News | Al Jazeera","content":"El Salvador has published a new law that will allow authorities to penalise minors as young as 12 with life imprisonment for severe crimes, including homicide, terrorism or rape.
On Tuesday, the Salvadoran government released the law, which is slated to take effect on April 26.
Since March 2022, El Salvador has been under a state of emergency that has suspended certain civil liberties in favour of greater police and military powers.
More than 90,000 people have been imprisoned.
Others have been processed in mass trials, a process approved in 2023 to allow up to 900 people to be tried at once.
The country’s legislative assembly is overwhelmingly controlled by Bukele’s Nuevas Ideas party.
“We shall see who supports this amendment, and who will dare to argue that the Constitution should continue to prohibit murderers and rapists from remaining in prison,” he wrote on March 17.
UNICEF warned that imprisonment could result in severe, long-term consequences on child and adolescent development, and added that such measures were unlikely to reduce crime overall."},{"url":"https://www.dailysabah.com/world/americas/el-salvador-allows-life-sentences-for-minors-under-new-reforms","title":"El Salvador allows life sentences for minors under new reforms | Daily Sabah","content":"El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele has enacted sweeping legal reforms allowing life imprisonment for minors as young as 12 convicted of serious crimes such as homicide, terrorism and rape, according to the country’s official gazette.
The measures, ⁠published on Tuesday and scheduled to take effect April 26, follow a constitutional amendment approved in March by the government-controlled Congress.
The constitutional amendment came days after an international legal panel reported "reasonable grounds" to suggest El Salvador has committed crimes against humanity during a years-long ⁠state of emergency.
By signing up you are agreeing to our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy."},{"url":"https://elsalvadorinenglish.com/2026/03/18/el-salvador-approves-life-sentences-for-murder-rape-and-terrorism/","title":"El Salvador Approves Life Sentences for Murder, Rape, and Terrorism – El Salvador in English","content":"The Legislative Assembly of El Salvador has approved a constitutional reform that would allow the implementation of life imprisonment for individuals convicted of serious crimes, including murder, rape, and acts of terrorism.
Authorities have also proposed complementary reforms to the Penal Code, the Juvenile Criminal Law, and legislation related to terrorism, aligning secondary laws with the proposed constitutional change.
According to Villatoro, despite external criticism, the country remains committed to protecting its citizens and upholding the rule of law."}]}
